narrative: As a Hispanic male applying to law school, you've built a solid academic foundation with your undergraduate GPA of 3.40, which stands above the typical 3.21 average, and an LSAT score of 39 that exceeds the usual 36.70 mark. Overall, these three elements—your solid Year 3 deciles helping, but family income and undergrad grades hindering—tipped the scales just over the edge. This isn't a judgment on your potential as a Hispanic man with your drive; it's the model being cautious.
